title: styphelia papillosa
intro: Styphelia papillosa, also known as the Tasmanian button-sedge or Tasmanian button bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. Styphelia papillosa is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of Tasmania, Australia, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. Styphelia papillosa can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health benefits.
